Name: Effortlessly Invariant Rulesbooks (ERB) - Project Architecture
Description: Top-level rulebook describing the ERB project itself: all moving parts, orchestration flows, substrates, and their interdependencies.

schema.name: Purpose
schema.Description: Demonstrate that a single declarative rulebook can generate working code in multiple execution substrates (Python, Go, SQL, etc.) that all produce identical results

schema.name: Architecture
schema.Description: Hub-and-spokes around Airtable; rulebook is the disposable IR

data.Name: Effortlessly Invariant Rulesbooks
data.Purpose: Multi-substrate code generation from declarative rulebooks
data.Architecture: Airtable (UI) → effortless-rulebook.json (IR) → Substrates (Postgres, Python, Go, Excel, OWL, etc.)

ExecutionSubstrates.Description: Runtime environments that execute business rules derived from the rulebook

schema.name: Technology
schema.Description: Language/platform (PostgreSQL, Python, Go, Excel, OWL, PlantUML, etc.)

schema.name: IsProduction
schema.Description: True if backed by an official Effortless tool; false if local implementation

data.Name: Binary (ARM64)
data.Description: ARM64 assembly proof-of-concept; demonstrates lowest-level code generation

data.Name: Python
data.Description: Local Python implementation: dataclasses + calculated field functions

data.Name: Go
data.Description: Local Go implementation: structs + business logic

data.Name: PostgreSQL
data.Description: Production tool: generates DDL, stored functions, views. Full formula support via SQL.

data.Name: PlantUML
data.Description: Generates UML class diagrams with OCL constraints

data.Name: Explain DAG
data.Description: JSON spec for calculated-field derivation tracing; embedded React visualizer

data.Name: Entity Framework
data.Description: Production tool: generates C# POCOs and Entity Framework DbContext

OrchestrationComponents.Description: Central orchestration logic that coordinates rulebook loading, injection, and testing

data.Name: Shared Utilities
data.Purpose: Common functions: load_rulebook(), parse formulas, type mapping

data.Name: Formula Parser
data.Purpose: Parse and execute Excel-style formulas (IF, AND, OR, CONCAT, LEFT, RIGHT, etc.)

data.Name: Main Injector
data.Purpose: Dispatch rulebook to all substrate injectors; optionally clean generated files

data.Name: Orchestrator Menu
data.Purpose: Interactive CLI menu: run substrates, manage Airtable sync, generate reports

data.Name: Rulebook Cache
data.Purpose: Pull rulebook from Airtable API; fallback to offline cache if unavailable

data.Name: Base Manager
data.Purpose: List, select, and swap Airtable bases; fetch base metadata from API

data.Name: Test Orchestrator
data.Purpose: Run all substrate tests; verify conformance (all substrates compute identically)

data.Name: Report Generator
data.Purpose: Generate HTML conformance report comparing substrate outputs

AirtableIntegration.Description: Airtable as input spoke: schema + data source pulled into rulebook

schema.name: Role
schema.Description: input (pull from Airtable), output (push to Airtable), or bidirectional

data.Name: airtable-to-rulebook CLI
data.Purpose: Official Effortless tool: exports schema + data from Airtable base to effortless-rulebook.json

data.Name: rulebook-to-airtable CLI
data.Purpose: Official Effortless tool: push formula updates back to Airtable (reverse sync)

data.Name: Rulebook Cache (offline mode)
data.Purpose: Cache previously-pulled rulebooks; use offline if Airtable API unavailable

data.Name: ACME Corporation Bases
data.Purpose: Demo Airtable bases for BASIC and ADVANCED ontologies; can be swapped at runtime

TestingFramework.Description: Conformance testing: prove all substrates compute identically

data.Name: Test Case Definitions
data.Purpose: YAML scenarios defining inputs and expected outputs; shared across all substrates

data.Name: Substrate-Specific Take-Test
data.Purpose: Load rulebook, execute test cases, compare results against expected

data.Name: Test Orchestrator
data.Purpose: Run all substrate tests; aggregate pass/fail; identify conformance gaps

data.Name: Conformance Report
data.Purpose: Visual comparison of substrate outputs; highlight mismatches

RulebookDomains.Description: Customer ontologies: each domain has its own rulebook + substrate generation

schema.name: RelativePath
schema.Description: rulebook-examples/{domain}/ — each domain is a self-contained Effortless project

schema.name: KeyFeatures
schema.Description: Comma-separated: string concat, relationships, aggregations, IF logic, meta-ontology, etc.

data.Purpose: Hello World: demonstrates basic schema and calculated field (CONCAT formula)
Real workflow modeling: task management with assigned agents

data.KeyFeatures: cross-entity lookups, conditional IF logic, complex aggregations
data.Purpose: Business rule complexity: demonstrates advanced formula patterns
Media catalog: TV shows, seasons, episodes; demonstrates polymorphism
Formal argument modeling: demonstrates schema expressivity for abstract domains

data.KeyFeatures: Hub promotion candidate; demonstrates rulebook-first workflow
data.Purpose: Enterprise demo: ACME Corp operations

data.KeyFeatures: Hub promotion candidate; demonstrates rulebook-first workflow
data.Purpose: Enterprise demo: ACME LLC operations

CoreDataFlows.Description: End-to-end flows from rulebook to execution and testing

data.Name: Standard Rulebook → Substrates
data.Steps: Load rulebook.json | Dispatch to all injectors | Generate substrate code | Package artifacts
data.Triggers: User selects option A in orchestrator menu
data.Outputs: Generated code in each execution-substrates/{tech}/ folder

data.Name: Pull from Airtable
data.Steps: Call airtable-to-rulebook CLI | Write effortless-rulebook.json | Cache offline version
data.Triggers: User selects option P (Pull & Inject) or changes base via option B

data.Name: Conformance Testing
data.Steps: Load test cases YAML | Run each substrate's take-test.py | Collect outputs | Compare results | Generate report
data.Triggers: User selects option V (View Results) or automatic after injection

data.Name: Clean Generated Files
data.Steps: Delete all generated outputs in each execution-substrates/ folder
data.Outputs: Empty substrate directories ready for fresh injection

data.Name: Swap Ontologies (Hub Promotion)
data.Steps: User selects domain in effortless-rulebooks/ | Point orchestrator to domain's rulebook | Re-run injection & tests with new domain
data.Triggers: Rulebook hub promotion complete; becomes the primary SSoT instead of Airtable
data.Outputs: All substrates regenerated for new domain; conformance verified

data.Purpose: SSoTme project definition: transpilers, versions, base IDs, API keys
Project instructions and architecture overview for Claude Code
Catalog of swappable Airtable bases with IDs, names, complexity levels
Environment variables: AIRTABLE_API_KEY, PostgreSQL credentials, etc.
Docker services: PostgreSQL, optional API server
Container image for reproducible environment
Core rulebook: entity schemas, field types, formulas, sample data
User-facing documentation: quick start, base progression, key terms
Technical deep-dive: architecture, internals, debugging

_meta._CMCC_Summary: Top-level rulebook describing the Effortlessly Invariant Rulesbooks (ERB) project itself: all execution substrates, orchestration components, Airtable integration, testing framework, customer domains, core data flows, configuration files, and external dependencies.
_meta._ProjectGoal: Demonstrate that a single declarative rulebook (effortless-rulebook.json) can generate working, conformant code in 10+ execution substrates (Python, Go, SQL, Excel, OWL, PlantUML, etc.) that all produce identical results for the same inputs.
_meta._ArchitecturalHighlight: Rulebook is the disposable IR; substrates are the execution targets. Hub-and-spokes around Airtable (or other sources like direct JSON edits). All 10+ substrates must pass the same conformance tests.
_meta._HubPromotionPhase: Transition from Airtable-as-SSoT to rulebook-as-SSoT. Customer domains (ACME Corp, ACME LLC) can be selected at runtime. Once complete, orchestration drives from effortless-rulebooks/<domain>/ instead of a single Airtable base.
